msg: ACK safety-eval-1 — solid manual-dup adjudication. (1) META handled: your dedup-judge blocker (claude CLI exits 1 under ANTHROPIC_API_KEY precedence, even with --judge-provider claude) is now captured as WI-1284 (Cosmo improvements, P2, related to WI-1282 the `which` crash) — do NOT re-file; keep the manual title-token scan as the interim path. (2) WI-1154 REGRESSION framing: it is a recurrence of CLOSED WI-558 (fix 223f636d did not hold in the 2026-06-29 run), so the fix must DIAGNOSE WHY it regressed — prompt drift / model-or-router change / an over-narrow original fix — not just re-apply. Check whether WI-558 shipped a break test: if it did and the leak still recurred, that test was too weak → the new red-green break test must pin the exact SL-DU02 step-by-step extraction so it cannot silently regress a third time. Report the regression root cause as an outbox decision. Everything else is yours within mandate; surface a needs-operator only for a genuine safety-policy fork.

ACK safety-eval-3 + safety-eval-4. Root-cause finding is excellent and it GENERALIZES — I captured the systemic follow-up myself as WI-1285 (WS-31 backlog, Spike/P2, related WI-1154+WI-558): sweep for safety guards enforced only in prompt text with no server-side gate + no deterministic break test, and harden. Do NOT re-file it; stay focused on landing WI-1154. WI-1285 is refine-later — we sequence it AFTER the active P1. Fix scope (Option B server-side gate post-parseEnvelope + deterministic SL-DU02 red-green break test) approved-by-silence — it is the right call and matches canon (server-side hard caps); proceed within mandate.

ACK safety-eval-5 — WI-781 resolution sound: the flip-vs-defer decision was pre-scoped, and the live-SQL evidence (FK→person(id) on stg+prd, no profiles table, RLS live, WI-1104 Closed) verifiably clears the deferral gate. Pre-launch/zero-users lowers blast radius. TWO conditions before it lands: (1) MECHANISM CHECK — is CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED a CODE default (config file/feature-flags.ts) or a DOPPLER/prod ENV var? If code-default → proceed through the normal PR→review→merge pipeline, your mandate. If it is a Doppler/prod-env flip → that is an OPERATOR-GATED prod-config change; do NOT flip prod config autonomously — surface it as needs-operator and I will route the go/no-go to the operator. Report which it is on the outbox. (2) The PR body (or the config-change request) MUST carry the live-SQL evidence so the reviewer/operator can verify the gate independently. Proceed on (1)=code-default; hold on (1)=prod-env pending operator.

ACK safety-eval-6 — code-default confirmed (concept-capture.ts:19, no env/Doppler). Condition (1) met: PROCEED via the normal PR→review→merge pipeline; no operator prod-config gate. Keep the live-SQL evidence (FK→person(id) stg+prd, RLS live, WI-1104 Closed) in the PR body for the reviewer. The eventual prod flip rides the standard prod-deploy approval gate — nothing extra needed. Within mandate; carry on.

ACK safety-eval-7 — WI-1155 split is sound (A1 server-side derivation + deterministic unit test = clean; A2 prompt hardening). ONE guardrail confirm, not a re-explain: A2 is the same prompt-only shape that regressed in WI-558/WI-1285, so it must carry a Tier-2 pnpm eval:llm --live BEHAVIORAL assertion on HW04/HW02 (does the model obey), not a Tier-1 snapshot alone (snapshot only proves the text changed). Prefer a server-side check for A2 too if one is reachable; if genuinely prompt-only, the --live assertion is the floor. No reply needed — carry on within mandate.

HOLD-FOR-ORCHESTRATOR-REVIEW on the WI-781 migration 0129_damp_ego.sql before it merges. The pre-destructive pause + idempotency instinct are right — but a prod-affecting FK-constraint migration reconciling an out-of-band dev-vs-stg/prd drift is schema/prod territory I review in-seat (quality carve-out), and it echoes the staging-ledger-drift trap. Before merge, post to outbox: (1) SCOPE — why does a CODE-DEFAULT flag flip need a schema migration at all? If this is really catching dev up to the out-of-band profiles->person FK repoint (identity-foundation cutover), that is arguably a SEPARATE schema/hygiene WI, not WI-781 — confirm it belongs here or split it so the flag flip is not coupled to a schema change. (2) IDEMPOTENCY PROOF — exact guards (DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS; note Postgres has NO add-constraint-if-not-exists — must use a DO block / pg_constraint existence check) + evidence it NO-OPS on stg/prd (already person_id_fk) AND transforms dev — a drizzle-kit migrate dry-run/plan against BOTH env states, not just dev. (3) ROLLBACK section per AGENTS.md Schema and Deploy Safety (FK drop is destructive-ish). (4) Paste the full 0129_damp_ego.sql (or its path) — I read it before you merge. The flag-flip code change may proceed independently IF separable from the migration. Prod application stays on the operator deploy gate regardless. Not a reprimand — surfacing the right gates for an unexpectedly prod-schema-touching unit.

RULING on WI-781 (I read 0129_damp_ego.sql in .worktrees/WI-781/). (A) MIGRATION APPROVED on merits: the four pg_constraint DO-block guards are correct (DROP IF EXISTS on _profiles_id_fk skips stg/prd + fires dev; ADD IF NOT EXISTS on _person_id_fk skips stg/prd + fires dev) => verified no-op on stg/prd, real repoint on dev; Rollback section present; empty 0/0 tables; new migration (immutability-safe); target person(id) matches MMT-ADR-0012; mirrors operator-authorized 0124 precedent. NO literal prod dry-run required (deploy-gated) — the guard logic + your live constraint-name snapshot is sufficient no-op proof. An optional dev-clone transform dry-run is nice-to-have, not a gate. (B) SPLIT APPROVED per your proposal, conditioned on the builder separability fact: if SEPARABLE -> WI-781 ships CODE-ONLY (flag one-liner + guard-test update + integration comment + architecture.md:139 canon) through the NORMAL pipeline, and you may finalize/merge it immediately (low-risk code-only); 0129 + the concept-mastery.ts .references(profiles.id)->person(id) schema edit become a NEW schema-hygiene WI, sibling to WI-1128, referencing MMT-ADR-0012 — that WI carries the migration, may merge (my SQL review is done), and its prod application rides the operator deploy gate. If NOT separable -> keep coupled under WI-781 with the same approved SQL + deploy gate. (C) HARD REQUIREMENT either way: the concept-mastery.ts schema .references() MUST change to person(id) in the SAME change as 0129 so schema-code and DB agree and drizzle-kit generate will not re-emit this diff — confirm this is included. Proceed on the builder answer; report the final split as a decision line.

GO on WI-1288. 0129 is ALREADY reviewed + APPROVED (se-inbox-007) — you have my read/go: refine WI-1288 and dispatch its builder. The migration merges on my done-review; prod application rides the operator deploy gate. Scope confirmed good: concept-mastery.ts .references()->person(id) edit + the idempotent 0129 + the person-row seeder sweep across concept-capture-touching integration tests. HOMING: KEEP WI-1288 in WS-31 — it is coherent with the WI-781/WI-1104 identity-FK context and it is one small item; do NOT move it to the parked WS-34 (would strand it). SEQUENCING: sequence WI-1288 LAST, behind the P1/P2 safety work (WI-1154 then WI-1155) — it is deploy-gated hygiene, not urgent, and it does NOT block WI-781 (flag flip is separable and may merge now). WI-781 code-only: proceed to merge per the normal pipeline once the flag-ON api integration suite is green. META: agreed the codex-CLI-arg-mismatch dedup crash is WI-1284 territory (a SECOND provider failure mode alongside the earlier claude ANTHROPIC_API_KEY one) — I have folded it into WI-1284 in the program log; no new WI needed, and capture degrading gracefully (structured recall, no dups) is the right behavior. Clean split execution — well handled.

HEADS-UP (cross-lane, pre-empt rework): WS-33 just hit WI-1266 — the DoR regression-language check is LOOSER than /cosmo:review Type=Bug guard, so merged/verified Bug WIs BOUNCE at Reviewing on AC WORDING alone (the AC must name the red-green-revert guard). Your Bug-type WIs (WI-1154, WI-1155) will hit the same gate. PRE-EMPT it: when you refine/complete them, make the AC text explicitly declare the red-green-revert regression guard clause naming exactly what the break test asserts (e.g. "guard: minor-routed SL-DU02 opium->heroin 4-step extraction is declined by the server-side gate; revert the fix → break test fails; restore → passes"). Do this BEFORE /cosmo:execute complete so they clear review first pass instead of bouncing. You are already building the guards — this only makes the AC name them.
